DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Taken Under Submisssion.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Continued To May-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .

1.Continued to May 6, 2025, per CCP 437c(h).
2.It appears from the declaration submitted in support of the opposition that facts necessary to justify opposition cannot be presented absent additional discovery.
3.Supplemental opposition and amended opposing separate statements are due on April 23, 2025.
4.Amended reply is due April 29, 2025
5.If suggested dates are not acceptable, please meet and confer and notify staff and dates will be changed. Do not appear on law and motion just to get new dates.

Visiting Judge: Barbara Zuniga, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/BZ)

Off Calendar Sep-28-2023 Continued To Oct-16-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Louis Beary, Esq. 707-495-4454 for Plaintiff.
Raymond Rollan, Esq. Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3888 for Defendant. (In-person)

Argued. Defense Counsel Mr. Rollan contests the tentative ruling. The Court inquires why he should consider the late Opposition. Mr Rollan responds. Mr. Beary requests to uphold its tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALEXANDRA BRIGGS' Motion To Be Relieved Of Government Claim.

Continued to 10/16/23 at 9:30am in Dept. 302. The City's 5-page brief on why late opposition should be considered is due by 10/3/23. In addition, Mr. Rollan may also address the issue on government claim requirements. Plaintiff's 10-page brief which replies to city's brief and city's opposition is due by 10/9/23.
